overview: Based on the published agenda, the Village of Weston Board of Trustees was scheduled to hold a regular meeting on June 15, 2026, to address routine village business including approval of various licenses and permits, consideration of three resolutions related to sewer compliance and street reconstruction projects, and discussion of several new business items including SAFER follow-up actions, community engagement efforts, ethics policies, and infrastructure projects. The meeting was also set to acknowledge reports from multiple boards, committees, and departments.
committee: Board of Trustees - Village of Weston

agenda.item: Approval of May 18, 2026 Board of Trustees Meeting minutes

Acknowledge reports and minutes from multiple boards, committees, and commissions including Community Development Authority, Community Life and Public Safety, Everest Metro, Extraterritorial Zoning, Finance and Human Resources, Joint Review Board, Mountain Bay Metropolitan Police, Parks & Recreation, Plan Commission, Public Works, SAFER, Tourism Commission, and Refuse and Recycling
Acknowledge departmental reports from Administrator, Clerk, Finance, Fire/EMS, Parks & Recreation, Plan/Development, Police, Public Works, and Technology
Acknowledge May Building Permits, May Budget Status, and May Code Enforcement Report
Approve Vouchers – check numbers 66741-66911 and 90252-90255
Renewal of Cigarette, Tobacco, and Electronic Vaping Licenses for the 2026-2027 Licensing Term
Renewal of Lodging License for the 2026-2027 Licensing Term
Renewal of Class A and Class B Fermented Malt Beverage and Intoxicating Liquor and Class C Wine Liquor Licenses for the 2026-2027 Licensing Term
Renewal of Mobile Home Park Licenses for the 2026-2027 Licensing Term
Renewal of Weights and Measures Licenses for the 2026-2027 Licensing Term
Appointment for Change of Agent for Reliance Fuel LLC d/b/a Fuel On 60
Resolution 2026-016 – Approval of the 2025 Compliance Maintenance Annual Report (CMAR) for Weston Sewer Utility and submission to the Wisconsin Department of Natural Resources
Resolution 2026-017 – Authorizing Resolution for Bloedel Ave (Pine Park St to Alderson St) Reconstruction
Resolution 2026-018 – Authorizing Resolution for Alderson St and Jelinek Ave Intersection Project
Discussion and/or action on Farmers Market Feedback Survey results to date and possible recommended follow-up actions
Discussion and/or action on Renewal of Hutch's Kelly Club LLC Class B Fermented Malt Beverage & Class B Intoxicating Liquor for the 2026-2027 licensing term
Discussion and/or action on Renewal of RJT & Sons LLC Class B Fermented Malt Beverage & Class B Intoxicating Liquor for the 2026-2027 licensing term
Discussion and/or action with SAFER following April Referendum result
Discussion and/or action on potential community engagement effort(s) and/or event(s)
Discussion and/or action on the Ethics function as it pertains to Village committees, commissions, and staff
Discussion and/or action on Topsoil Screening Quotes
Discussion and/or action on Shirley Avenue Paving Quotes
Discussion and/or action on Contract Amendment No. 1 with MSA for Alderson Street and Jelinek Avenue Right-of-Way Services
Discussion and/or action on Change Order No. 1 for Alderson Street and Jelinek Avenue Project
Well 4 Safe Drinking Water Loan Program Financial Assistance Agreement Amendment No. 1
Discussion and/or action on Bellewood Avenue Watermain Replacement Project

discussions.body: The Board was scheduled to discuss the results to date from the Farmers Market Feedback Survey and consider possible recommended follow-up actions based on the feedback received.

discussions.item: Renewal of Hutch's Kelly Club LLC Class B Fermented Malt Beverage & Intoxicating Liquor License
discussions.body: The Board was set to consider renewal of the Class B Fermented Malt Beverage and Class B Intoxicating Liquor license for Hutch's Kelly Club LLC for the 2026-2027 licensing term.

discussions.item: Renewal of RJT & Sons LLC Class B Fermented Malt Beverage & Intoxicating Liquor License
discussions.body: The Board was set to consider renewal of the Class B Fermented Malt Beverage and Class B Intoxicating Liquor license for RJT & Sons LLC for the 2026-2027 licensing term.

discussions.item: SAFER Discussion Following April Referendum Result
discussions.body: The Board was scheduled to discuss and potentially take action with SAFER regarding follow-up to the results of an April referendum affecting the fire service organization.
The Board was set to discuss and consider potential community engagement effort(s) and/or event(s) for the Village.

discussions.item: Ethics Function for Village Committees, Commissions, and Staff
discussions.body: The Board was scheduled to discuss and potentially take action on the ethics function as it pertains to Village committees, commissions, and staff.
The Board was set to review and potentially take action on topsoil screening quotes for a village project.
The Board was scheduled to review and potentially take action on paving quotes for Shirley Avenue.

discussions.item: Contract Amendment No. 1 with MSA for Alderson Street and Jelinek Avenue Right-of-Way Services
discussions.body: The Board was set to consider a contract amendment with MSA for right-of-way services related to the Alderson Street and Jelinek Avenue intersection project.

discussions.item: Change Order No. 1 for Alderson Street and Jelinek Avenue Project
discussions.body: The Board was scheduled to consider a change order for the Alderson Street and Jelinek Avenue intersection project.
The Board was set to discuss and potentially take action on the Bellewood Avenue Watermain Replacement Project.

publicComment: Public comment was indicated on the agenda for any person wishing to address the Board for up to three minutes regarding a non-agenda item, with time extension permitted per the Chief Presiding Officer's discretion.

title: Board of Trustees - 6/15/2026
